title: FEA Youth Steering Group – Applications Open
summary: Applications are now open for the next cohort of the FEA Youth Steering Group , a leadership opportunity for young advocates passionate about advancing education equity. About the Programme The Youth Steering Group empowers young people to influence education policy, advocate for fairer systems, and shape conversations around educational access and equity. Previous cohort members have: Engaged in a roundtable with the Prime Minister Contributed to policy discussions with the Department for Education Taken on leadership roles across the education sector Who Should Apply This opportunity is for young people who: Are aged 14–21 Are passionate about education equity Have experienced barriers to fair education Want to use their voice to advocate for change Key Details Programme: FEA Youth Steering Group Application Deadline: 28 June 2026 Benefits of Joining Develop leadership and advocacy skills Influence education policy and sector conversations Build networks with peers and decision-makers Gain experience in public speaking and representation Why This Opportunity Matters This programme gives young people a platform to shape the future of education and ensure lived experiences inform policy and practice. How to Apply Learn more and apply here For more Training Opportunities, Visit OFY here. The post FEA Youth Steering Group – Applications Open appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Climate Justice Camp 2026 Asia in the Philippines: Fully Funded Regional Gathering for Climate Leaders and Community Organizers
summary: The Climate Justice Camp 2026 Asia is set to take place in Cebu, Philippines, from August 12 to 16, 2026, bringing together grassroots organizers, activists, and community leaders from across Asia and the Pacific. This transformative initiative is one of the largest in-person organizing platforms globally, designed to empower individuals working at the intersection of climate justice, human rights, and social equity. Launched in 2022, the Climate Justice Camp has rapidly grown into a global movement, with nearly 2,000 participants from over 100 countries engaging in collaborative learning, advocacy, and action. The 2026 Asia edition marks a significant milestone as the first regional camp hosted in Asia, offering a unique opportunity for regional voices to shape climate justice solutions. What is the Climate Justice Camp? The Climate Justice Camp is an annual global gathering designed to create a space where community organizers can: Build meaningful relationships and networks Share experiences, knowledge, and best practices Develop innovative strategies for climate action Strengthen grassroots movements and advocacy efforts It is a collaborative, participant-driven platform that emphasizes local knowledge, collective care, and transformative change. Event Details Location: Cebu, Philippines Dates: August 12–16, 2026 Arrival Date: August 11, 2026 Departure Date: August 17, 2026 The camp runs for a full week, and participants are expected to attend all sessions. What the Camp Offers Participants will gain access to a rich and immersive experience designed to foster learning, collaboration, and action. Key Features Workshops and Trainings Skill-building sessions led by experts and participants Topics include climate justice, advocacy, organizing, and policy Collaborative Learning Peer-to-peer knowledge sharing Exchange of ideas across diverse communities Networking Opportunities Connect with activists, leaders, and organizations Build long-term partnerships and alliances Strategy Development Co-create solutions to pressing climate challenges Develop actionable plans for local implementation Healing and Reflection Spaces Focus on resilience, well-being, and collective care Safe space for sharing experiences and challenges Financial Support and Costs The Climate Justice Camp ensures accessibility for participants by covering several key costs: Covered Expenses Accommodation Meals Local transportation within Cebu Participant Responsibilities Travel costs to Cebu Visa-related expenses (if required) Limited travel sponsorship is available and will be assessed on a case-by-case basis. Approved travel support will be reimbursed upon arrival. Eligibility Criteria The program is open to individuals who meet the following conditions: Must reside in the Asia region or be part of the Asian diaspora Residents of Pacific Island countries are also eligible Actively engaged in community organizing or advocacy work Working on issues such as: Climate justice Environmental protection Human rights Women’s rights Indigenous rights Water rights and sustainability Applicants are encouraged to apply as individuals or with members of their organization or community. Eligible Countries Applicants can apply from a wide range of countries across Asia and the Pacific, including but not limited to: South Asia: India, Pakistan, Nepal, Bangladesh, Sri Lanka Southeast Asia: Indonesia, Philippines, Vietnam, Thailand, Malaysia East Asia: China, Japan, South Korea Central Asia: Kazakhstan, Uzbekistan, Kyrgyzstan Pacific Islands: Fiji, Samoa, Tonga, Vanuatu, and others A comprehensive list of eligible countries is provided in the official application details. Application Deadline Deadline: May 31, 2026 (23:59 UTC+5) Applicants are encouraged to apply early due to the high volume of submissions. APPLY HERE Selection Timeline Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis Selected participants will be notified directly If no response is received by July 10, 2026, applicants should consider their application unsuccessful Why You Should Apply Participating in the Climate Justice Camp offers unparalleled opportunities to: Engage with leading climate activists and organizers Strengthen your skills in advocacy and organizing Contribute to global and regional climate solutions Build networks that extend beyond the camp Amplify your impact within your community Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 1. What is the Climate Justice Camp? It is a global platform for grassroots organizers to collaborate, learn, and develop strategies for climate and social justice. 2. Who can apply? Community organizers, activists, and leaders working on justice-related issues in Asia, the Pacific, or the Asian diaspora. 3. Can I apply with my team? Yes. Group applications are encouraged, but each member must submit an individual application. 4. Is the program fully funded? The camp covers accommodation, meals, and local transport. Travel and visa costs are the responsibility of participants, though limited funding is available. 5. Do I need to attend all days? Yes. Full participation is mandatory for all selected participants. 6. When will I know the results? Applicants will be contacted directly if selected or waitlisted. No response by July 10 indicates non-selection. 7. What should I expect during the camp? Workshops, networking, collaborative sessions, and opportunities to co-create solutions for climate justice. 8. Who organizes the camp? The initiative is hosted by Roots, in collaboration with over 200 global and regional partner organizations. Click HERE to learn more about the Climate Justice Camp 2026 Asia. Conclusion The Climate Justice Camp 2026 Asia is a powerful platform for emerging and experienced leaders to come together, share knowledge, and drive meaningful change. It represents a unique opportunity to strengthen grassroots movements and contribute to a more just and sustainable future. Discover more global Climate Action and Conference opportunities on OFY. The post Climate Justice Camp 2026 Asia in the Philippines: Fully Funded Regional Gathering for Climate Leaders and Community Organizers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: International Paralympic Committee Classification Research Grant (2026–2028)
summary: The International Paralympic Committee has launched the second call for its Classification Research Grant , supporting multidisciplinary research that strengthens evidence-based classification systems across Para sports. About the Grant This funding opportunity supports research designed to improve fairness, transparency, and scientific rigor in Para sport classification. Projects should contribute to the development of robust classification systems that enhance competitive equity across Paralympic sports. Funding Details Grant Amount: €30,000 – €150,000 Project Duration: 12–24 months Programme Period: 2026–2028 Who Can Apply Eligible applicants include: International Federations National Paralympic Committees Research Institutions Collaborative partnerships between academic institutions and sport bodies Application Process The grant follows a two-stage application process , beginning with submission of a short project summary. Key Deadline Phase 1 Deadline: 31 May 2026 Why This Opportunity Matters This is a valuable opportunity for researchers and institutions working at the intersection of: Sport science Disability inclusion Performance research Evidence-based policy in sport It supports efforts to build fairer and more scientifically grounded Para sport systems worldwide. How to Apply Learn more and apply here For more Grant opportunities, Visit OFY here. title: Recourse Is Hiring an Advisor in Africa to Support Just Energy Transition Advocacy
summary: Global advocacy organization Recourse is hiring an Advisor based in Africa to support its research, advocacy, and campaigns focused on international financial institutions and just energy transitions. This is an exciting opportunity for professionals passionate about climate justice, development finance, and sustainable energy systems to contribute to global advocacy efforts while working remotely within an international team. Although the preferred location is Kenya, the role is open to candidates based across Africa and has a global scope. About the Role The Advisor will support Recourse’s strategic work influencing international financial institutions and advocating for equitable energy transition policies in the Global South. The successful candidate will collaborate with allies and partners across multiple regions, contributing to research, campaigns, and policy engagement initiatives. Key Responsibilities Responsibilities are expected to include: Supporting research on development finance and international financial institutions Contributing to advocacy and campaign strategies Engaging with partners and stakeholders across regions Advancing work on just energy transitions in Global South contexts Collaborating with remote global teams Undertaking occasional international travel (up to four weeks per year) Candidate Profile Recourse is seeking applicants with: Strong understanding of development finance Expertise in energy transitions , especially in Global South contexts Experience in advocacy, research, or campaigning Ability to work effectively in distributed international teams Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills Location and Work Arrangement Primary Base: Africa Preferred Location: Kenya Work Style: Remote Travel Requirement: Up to four weeks annually Application Deadline No deadline has been specified. Early application is recommended. How to Apply To view the full job description and application process, visit here. For more Job opportunities, Visit OFY here. title: Next Generation Japan Studies Development Program 2026: Funding, Mentorship, and Global Research Opportunities
summary: The Social Science Research Council (SSRC) , in partnership with the Japan Foundation , is offering an exceptional opportunity for emerging scholars through the Next Generation Japan Studies Development Program . This initiative is designed to strengthen the field of Japan Studies by supporting early-career researchers, doctoral candidates, and scholars committed to advancing knowledge on Japan’s society, culture, politics, and economy. At a time when global understanding of Japan is increasingly important, this program provides critical funding, mentorship, and academic development opportunities to help shape the next generation of experts in the field. About the Program The Next Generation Japan Studies Development Program is a strategic initiative aimed at cultivating a vibrant and diverse community of scholars focused on Japan. The program seeks to expand the global reach of Japan Studies while fostering innovative and interdisciplinary research. Through this program, participants gain access to: Financial support for research projects Professional development resources Opportunities for academic networking and collaboration Mentorship from established scholars in the field The initiative emphasizes long-term academic growth, ensuring that participants are not only supported financially but also intellectually and professionally. Program Objectives The program is built around several key goals: Strengthening Japan Studies Globally It aims to expand the number of scholars specializing in Japan across different regions and disciplines. Supporting Early-Career Researchers By targeting doctoral students and emerging scholars, the program ensures sustained growth in the field. Encouraging Interdisciplinary Research Applicants are encouraged to explore Japan through various lenses, including social sciences, humanities, economics, and policy studies. Promoting Academic Collaboration The program fosters connections between scholars, institutions, and research communities worldwide. What the Program Offers Participants selected for the program benefit from a wide range of support mechanisms designed to enhance their academic journey: 1. Research Funding Financial assistance to support fieldwork, data collection, and research-related activities Enables scholars to focus on producing high-quality, impactful research 2. Mentorship Opportunities Guidance from experienced academics and experts in Japan Studies Support in refining research questions, methodologies, and outputs 3. Professional Development Access to workshops, seminars, and training sessions Opportunities to develop academic writing, publishing, and presentation skills 4. Networking and Collaboration Engagement with a global community of Japan Studies scholars Opportunities to participate in conferences and collaborative projects Research Focus Areas The program supports a wide range of research topics related to Japan, including but not limited to: Japanese society and culture Political systems and governance Economic development and policy International relations involving Japan Historical and contemporary issues shaping Japan Applicants are encouraged to propose innovative research that contributes new insights to the field. Eligibility and Participation The program is specifically designed for individuals who demonstrate a strong commitment to Japan Studies and academic excellence. While detailed eligibility requirements are outlined on the official program page, the initiative generally targets: Doctoral students conducting research on Japan Early-career scholars seeking to advance their academic work Researchers affiliated with academic or research institutions The program also prioritizes diversity and aims to include participants from various regions to ensure a broad and inclusive representation of perspectives. Application Process To Apply for the Next Generation Japan Studies Development Program, applicants are required to: Prepare a detailed research proposal outlining their project Demonstrate the relevance of their work to Japan Studies Provide academic credentials and supporting documents Submit their application through the official SSRC platform Applicants are strongly encouraged to carefully review the full program guidelines and requirements before submission. Deadline Applicants should refer to the official program page for the most up-to-date deadline information and ensure timely submission of all required materials. Why You Should Apply This program stands out as a unique opportunity for scholars looking to deepen their expertise in Japan Studies. Key benefits include: Access to funding that enables meaningful research Mentorship that supports academic and professional growth Exposure to global academic networks Opportunities to contribute to a growing and impactful field of study By participating in this program, scholars position themselves at the forefront of research on Japan, gaining valuable experience and recognition in the academic community. To discover more more global fellowships on OFY, click here The post Next Generation Japan Studies Development Program 2026: Funding, Mentorship, and Global Research Opportunities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Master Data Analysis with MITx: Learn R, Statistics, and Real-World Research Skills for Free
summary: In today’s data-driven world, the ability to analyze and interpret information is no longer optional—it is essential. The Data Analysis for Social Scientists course offers a powerful opportunity for learners to build strong analytical skills and apply them to real-world social, economic, and policy challenges. Whether you are a student, researcher, policymaker, or simply curious about data, this course provides a structured and practical pathway into modern data analysis. About the Course This course introduces learners to the core principles of probability and statistics , forming the backbone of data analysis. It goes beyond theory by incorporating real-world applications and frontier research , ensuring that participants not only understand concepts but also learn how to apply them effectively. A key feature of the course is hands-on training using the R statistical programming language , one of the most widely used tools in data science. Learners will engage in self-directed empirical analyses, allowing them to build practical skills that are immediately applicable in academic, professional, and policy environments. Additionally, the course can be taken as a standalone learning experience or as part of the MITx MicroMasters Program in Data, Economics, and Design of Policy (DEDP) , which offers a pathway toward advanced studies at MIT. What You Will Learn The course covers a wide range of topics designed to equip learners with both foundational knowledge and advanced analytical techniques: Introduction to Data Analysis in R Learn how to manipulate, analyze, and visualize data using R. Fundamentals of Probability Understand random variables, probability distributions, and statistical reasoning. Data Collection and Description Explore methods for gathering, summarizing, and interpreting data. Probability Distributions Study joint, marginal, and conditional distributions and their applications. Statistical Inference Learn about estimation, confidence intervals, and hypothesis testing. Causality and Experimental Design Analyze randomized experiments and understand causal relationships. Regression Analysis Work with single and multivariate linear models. Advanced Econometric Concepts Explore endogeneity, instrumental variables, and omitted variable bias. Machine Learning and Visualization Gain exposure to modern techniques for analyzing and presenting data. These topics ensure that learners develop a well-rounded understanding of both theoretical and applied data analysis . Course Structure and Flexibility The course is designed with flexibility in mind: Free to audit : Learners can access all course materials at no cost. Optional certificate : Participants can take a proctored exam and earn a certificate for a fee, which varies based on ability to pay. Self-paced learning : Allows individuals to learn at their own convenience. This makes the course accessible to a wide audience regardless of financial or time constraints. Prerequisites While no prior knowledge of probability or statistics is required, learners are expected to have: Basic familiarity with algebra Some understanding of calculus To help learners prepare, a course readiness check is available, enabling participants to assess whether they need to review foundational concepts before starting. Who Should Take This Course? This course is ideal for: Students pursuing social sciences, economics, or public policy Researchers looking to strengthen their data analysis skills Professionals working with data in government, NGOs, or private sectors Anyone interested in understanding and communicating data-driven insights Apply Here Important Enrollment Information Due to regulatory restrictions, learners residing in certain regions—including Iran, Cuba, North Korea, and specific regions of Ukraine—are not eligible to register for this course. Why This Course Matters Data plays a critical role in shaping decisions across industries and governments. By completing this course, learners will: Develop strong analytical and statistical skills Gain hands-on experience with R programming Learn to interpret and communicate complex data Build a foundation for advanced studies or careers in data science This course is more than just an introduction—it is a gateway to mastering data analysis in the social sciences . Learn more about this opportunity here To discover more global short course on OFY, click here The post Master Data Analysis with MITx: Learn R, Statistics, and Real-World Research Skills for Free appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Asia-Pacific WEPs Awards 2026: A Prestigious Opportunity to Champion Gender Equality in Business
summary: The 2026 Asia-Pacific Women’s Empowerment Principles (WEPs) Awards are officially open for applications, offering organizations across the region a powerful platform to showcase their commitment to gender equality and women’s empowerment in the workplace, marketplace, and community. Organized under the framework of the Women’s Empowerment Principles (WEPs), this initiative recognizes forward-thinking businesses and leaders who are driving meaningful change and setting benchmarks for inclusive and equitable practices. Key Dates and Timeline Applications Open: 21 April 2026 Application Deadline: 30 June 2026 Shortlisting Notification: July 2026 (via email) National Awards Ceremonies: October – November 2026 Held in Cambodia, China, India, Indonesia, Malaysia, Pakistan, Philippines, Thailand, and Vietnam Regional Awards Ceremony: Early December 2026 About the Asia-Pacific WEPs Awards The WEPs Awards celebrate organizations that demonstrate outstanding efforts in advancing gender equality aligned with the Women’s Empowerment Principles. These awards highlight innovative policies, impactful programs, and measurable progress toward empowering women. Participating in the awards offers organizations an opportunity to: Gain regional and international recognition Showcase best practices and successful initiatives Strengthen credibility and visibility Inspire other organizations to adopt gender-inclusive policies Who Can Apply? Organizations from across the Asia-Pacific region are invited to apply. Applicants can submit one application and choose to compete in up to two award categories , depending on their initiatives and impact. Applications must be submitted online, primarily in English. However, for selected countries hosting national awards, applications may also be submitted in local languages. Application Structure and Evaluation Criteria The application process is structured into three main parts: 1. Applicant Information Basic organizational details and background information. 2. General Questions (25% of total score) Multiple-choice questions Designed to assess alignment with WEPs principles Focus on company policies and gender equality practices 3. Category-Specific Questions (75% of total score) Open-ended questions Require detailed descriptions of initiatives or programs Applicants must demonstrate impact, innovation, and sustainability Supporting Documents Requirements Applicants are encouraged to provide documentation to support their responses. While not mandatory, these documents significantly strengthen applications. Recommended materials include: HR and business policies Annual reports Sustainability reports Program or initiative-specific reports Articles, photos, videos, and other multimedia For start-ups or smaller organizations lacking formal documentation, a well-written descriptive explanation is acceptable. All submitted materials will remain confidential and used solely for evaluation purposes. How to Apply Follow these steps to submit your application: Select your preferred language and begin the online application Save your progress and return later if needed (via JotForm system) Complete all sections of the application Submit your application online Receive a confirmation email upon successful submission Note: The “save progress” feature is not available for applications submitted through China-specific forms. Why You Should Apply Participating in the WEPs Awards is more than just recognition—it is a strategic move for organizations committed to sustainable development and inclusive growth. Key benefits include: Recognition as a leader in gender equality Enhanced brand reputation and trust Opportunities for regional networking and partnerships Contribution to Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) , particularly SDG 5 (Gender Equality) Final Thoughts The Asia-Pacific WEPs Awards 2026 present a unique opportunity for organizations to highlight their commitment to empowering women and advancing equality. Whether you are a multinational corporation, SME, or start-up, your efforts can inspire change and shape a more inclusive future. Make sure to submit your application before 30 June 2026 and take your place among the region’s most impactful organizations. Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Awards opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post Asia-Pacific WEPs Awards 2026: A Prestigious Opportunity to Champion Gender Equality in Business appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Forrest Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026–2027: Fully Funded Research Opportunity in Australia for Outstanding Early-Career Scholars
summary: The Forrest Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026–2027 , offered by the Forrest Research Foundation , represent one of the most prestigious and competitive research opportunities for early-career scholars worldwide. Designed to attract exceptional postdoctoral researchers, this fellowship supports individuals who demonstrate academic excellence, intellectual curiosity, and a commitment to making a meaningful global impact through innovative research. With generous financial support, world-class research environments, and strong mentorship opportunities, the Forrest Fellowship empowers researchers to push boundaries, challenge conventions, and contribute to interdisciplinary knowledge creation. About the Forrest Postdoctoral Fellowships The Forrest Fellowship is tailored for early-stage postdoctoral researchers who have already shown exceptional academic performance and research potential. Candidates are expected to: Be among the top-performing students in their undergraduate and graduate studies Have made significant contributions to research within and beyond their discipline Demonstrate innovation and originality in their doctoral work Show a willingness to explore interdisciplinary approaches Be committed to communicating research impact to society Forrest Fellows are not only researchers but also thought leaders who embrace unconventional approaches, collaborate across disciplines, and actively contribute to solving global challenges. Key Benefits of the Fellowship The Forrest Research Foundation provides a comprehensive funding package to support fellows throughout their research journey: 1. Competitive Salary Salary aligned with Level A Step 8 academic appointment Example: Approximately $112,371 per year (2026 rate) Includes an additional 17% superannuation contribution 2. Accommodation Allowance Annual allowance of $25,740 (2026 rate) Helps cover living expenses in Perth, Australia 3. Research and Travel Funding One-time $12,000 allowance Can be used for: Research-related expenses International conferences Overseas research collaborations 4. Relocation Support Available for both interstate and international fellows May include: Economy airfare to Perth Additional relocation assistance (subject to discussion) 5. Academic Advancement Opportunities Possibility of Level B appointment depending on qualifications and university support Access to leading academic institutions and research networks Additional Responsibilities and Opportunities Beyond research, Forrest Fellows are expected to actively contribute to the broader mission of the foundation by: Providing mentorship to Forrest Scholars Serving as ambassadors and leaders within the research community Engaging in activities at Forrest Hall Promoting collaboration and intellectual exchange Eligibility Criteria To be eligible for the 2026 application round (for a 2027 start), applicants must meet the following requirements: Be in the final year of a PhD or Be within two years of PhD completion Specifically: Applicants must have received their PhD on or after 1 January 2024 , OR Must complete all PhD requirements by 31 December 2026 The fellowship is open to candidates from diverse academic disciplines, with no strict limitation on fields of study. Application Process Applicants must prepare and submit a comprehensive application package, including: Completed Application Form Curriculum Vitae (CV) List of Publications List of Grants and Awards Contact Details of Three Referees At least two must be academic referees Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the full fellowship guidelines before submission: https://forrestresearch.org.au/fellowship-guidelines/ Application Timeline Applications Currently Closed Next Application Round Opens: May 2026 Fellowship Start Date: 2027 Applicants are advised to begin preparing early, including identifying referees and refining research proposals. Why Apply for the Forrest Fellowship? The Forrest Postdoctoral Fellowship is more than financial support—it is a platform for transformative research and leadership development. Successful applicants gain: Access to cutting-edge research environments in Australia Opportunities to collaborate globally Recognition as part of an elite community of scholars The ability to expand research beyond doctoral work A chance to influence policy, innovation, and global development Final Thoughts If you are an ambitious early-career researcher ready to take your work to the next level, the Forrest Postdoctoral Fellowship offers the ideal environment to grow, innovate, and make a lasting impact. With its strong emphasis on excellence, collaboration, and societal contribution, this fellowship stands out as a gateway to global research leadership. Prepare ahead of the May 2026 application opening to maximize your chances of success. Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post Forrest Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026–2027: Fully Funded Research Opportunity in Australia for Outstanding Early-Career Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Fully Funded PhD in Human Rights, Global Politics and International Studies (Italy) 2026–2027
summary: If you are passionate about shaping global conversations on human rights, governance, and international affairs, this prestigious doctoral opportunity in Italy offers a powerful platform to advance your academic and professional journey. The PhD in Human Rights, Global Politics and International Studies at Scuola Universitaria Superiore Sant’Anna in Pisa is now open for applications for the 2026–2027 academic cycle. This program is designed for highly motivated graduates who want to engage in interdisciplinary research and contribute to global policy debates. With full funding, international exposure, and extensive academic support, this PhD stands out as one of Europe’s top opportunities in the field. Overview of the PhD Programme The PhD program provides a rigorous, interdisciplinary, and international research environment , combining political science, international relations, law, and human rights studies. Key Features: Duration: 3 years (fully funded) Location: Pisa, Italy Research Focus: Human Rights Global Politics International Studies Language of Instruction: English Funding and Benefits This is a fully funded PhD program , offering generous financial and academic support: Financial Support: Annual scholarship: €19,740 (gross) Monthly net stipend: approximately €1,600 Additional housing allowance: €2,200 per year Additional Benefits: 26 free meals per month at the university canteen 50% increase in scholarship during research periods abroad Personal research budget (10% of the scholarship amount) Access to a vibrant academic and research community Available Positions 5 fully funded positions currently available Additional positions may be offered depending on external funding Application Requirements To successfully apply, candidates must submit a complete application with all required documents. Mandatory Documents: Valid identity document Academic qualification certificate (or proof of expected completion) Transcript of records (list of examinations taken) Curriculum Vitae (CV) Motivation letter Research project (must be written in English) I mportant: Failure to submit any mandatory document will result in disqualification. Optional Documents: Additional academic qualifications or publications Up to 3 academic reference letters Referees must submit letters within 3 days after the application deadline MSc thesis or abstract (or draft if not yet completed) Programme Structure and Academic Experience The PhD offers a dynamic structure that supports academic excellence and innovation: Advanced coursework tailored to research areas Close supervision by expert faculty members Opportunities for international collaboration Access to academic conferences and workshops Strong emphasis on independent research and publication Important Dates Application Deadline: May 15, 2026 (12:00 PM CEST) Interview Schedule: To be announced Final Ranking List: By July 15, 2026 How to Apply Click here for more and to apply Candidates are encouraged to carefully review all requirements and prepare their documents in advance. Why Choose This PhD? This program is ideal for individuals who want to: Build a career in academia, international organizations, or policy-making Conduct impactful research on global issues Join a prestigious European institution Gain international exposure and professional networks For more global scholarship opportunities Click here The post Fully Funded PhD in Human Rights, Global Politics and International Studies (Italy) 2026–2027 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Join the IHDCYH Youth Advisory Council in Canada (2026) – Shape the Future of Health Research
summary: Are you a young person passionate about health, well-being, and making a real difference in your community? The IHDCYH Youth Advisory Council (YAC) , an initiative of the Canadian Institutes of Health Research, is now accepting applications for 2026! This is a unique opportunity for youth across Canada to directly influence health research, policies, and programs that impact communities nationwide. If you want your voice heard at a national level and collaborate with like-minded peers, this is your chance. What is the IHDCYH Youth Advisory Council? The Youth Advisory Council (YAC) is a diverse group of young individuals brought together by the Institute of Human Development, Child and Youth Health to ensure youth perspectives are included in health-related decision-making. Core Purpose: Amplify youth voices in health research and policy Provide input on priority areas affecting children and youth Represent diverse communities across Canada Contribute to meaningful discussions shaping the future of healthcare What Will You Do as a Council Member? As a member of the Youth Advisory Council, you will actively participate in shaping national conversations on health and well-being. Key Responsibilities: Participate in virtual meetings and discussions Share insights on health-related issues affecting youth Collaborate with other youth from diverse backgrounds Provide feedback on policies, research priorities, and initiatives Represent your community’s unique perspectives Why You Should Apply Joining the YAC is more than just a volunteer role—it’s a transformative leadership experience. Benefits of Joining: Influence national health policies and research Connect with youth leaders across Canada Gain exposure to the health research ecosystem Build leadership, advocacy, and communication skills Contribute to equity, diversity, and inclusion in healthcare Participate in meaningful discussions that impact real-world outcomes Diversity and Representation The council values diverse lived experiences and perspectives . Members come from different regions, cultures, and backgrounds, including: Students and early-career researchers Youth advocates and community leaders Individuals with lived healthcare experiences Indigenous, rural, and underserved community representatives This diversity ensures that decisions reflect the realities of youth across Canada. Application Details Eligibility Requirements: Must be living in Canada Must be aged 12 to 25 years Application Components: While the exact application requirements may vary, candidates are encouraged to: Demonstrate interest in health, well-being, or research Share personal experiences or perspectives Highlight leadership, volunteer, or advocacy involvement Important Deadline Application Deadline: May 19, 2026 Click here for more and to apply Applicants are encouraged to apply early and ensure all required information is submitted before the deadline. Additional Information Applicants can explore: FAQs about the program Past Youth Advisory Council activities and impact reports Alumni network and engagement opportunities Mailing list for future youth engagement initiatives Why This Opportunity Matters Youth are at the center of shaping a healthier future. By joining the IHDCYH Youth Advisory Council, you’re not just participating—you’re actively contributing to decisions that influence health systems, research priorities, and policies across Canada. This is your opportunity to: Be heard Be involved Be impactful For more global fellowship opportunities Click here The post Join the IHDCYH Youth Advisory Council in Canada (2026) – Shape the Future of Health Research appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
